channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Donald Glover Voicing Yoshi in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
item.content:encoded:
Several new cast members have been named for The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, releasing on April 1.

On March 9, Nintendo aired the final trailer for The Super Mario Galaxy Movie as part of a special Nintendo Direct celebrating Mario Day (MAR10 Day). The final trailer revealed more plot details and also shed light on a villain coming to the big screen who hasn’t officially been seen since the US release of Super Mario Bros. 2 in 1986: Wart.

After the end of the trailer, Chris Meledandri from Illumination announced that Wart would be voiced by Luis Guzmán. He also revealed that Issa Rae will be playing the unseen Honey Queen from the Super Mario Galaxy games. The final announcement was that Yoshi, revealed in a previous trailer, would be voiced by Donald Glover.

Nintendo Today App Integration With Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Shigeru Miyamoto also announced that fans will be able to check in to see the Super Mario Galaxy Movie in theaters by using the Nintendo Today app. There will also be new wallpapers and other tie-ins to get fans excited for the movie.

Beyond that, this is the main event of the Super Mario Bros 40th anniversary celebration. Nintendo is holding some other deals to celebrate Mario Day. Learn more information about those deals here.

How do you feel about the casting choices for the Super Mario Galaxy Movie? Will you be seeing the movie in theaters on release day? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Valve Hit With Class Action Suit Over Alleged Loot Box Gambling

A class action lawsuit has been filed against Valve, accusing the PC gaming giant of running an illegal gambling operation.

Until Dawn Remake Developer Ballistic Moon Officially Dissolved

Until Dawn Remake developer Ballistic Moon has officially been dissolved. Paperwork was filed with UK government in February 2026.

EA Makes Layoffs Across All Battlefield Studios in ‘Realignment’ Bid

Electronic Arts has hit Battlefield Studios (including Criterion, Motive, DICE, and Ripple Effect) with a wave of layoffs.

Resident Evil Director Shinji Mikami Developing New AAA Game

Resident Evil director Shinji Mikami is leading development of a new AAA game at Unbound Games for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.



https://insider-gaming.com/donald-glover-voicing-yoshi-in-the-super-mario-galaxy-movie/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Hit With Class Action Suit Over Alleged Loot Box Gambling
item.content:encoded:
A class action lawsuit has been filed against Valve, accusing the PC gaming giant of running an illegal gambling operation through loot boxes in video games.

The lawsuit was filed on March 9, 2026, in the US District Court for the Western District of Washington. Valve is being accused of knowingly operating an unlawful gambling system. The lawsuit also alleges that loot boxes were carefully engineered to extract money from customers, including children, through deceptive psychological tactics.

Hagens Berman is the firm representing the proposed class of US consumers against Valve. Steve Berman, Hagens Berman’s founder and managing partner, states that Valve stacked the odds against consumers seeking entertainment. Now, they “intend to hold Valve accountable and put money back in the pockets of consumers.”

Valve Was Recently Hit With a Similar Complaint

The state of New York is also suing Valve over the gambling practices created by its loot box system. Counter-Strike 2 and DOTA 2 are mentioned in both filings. Hagens Berman also has a website where consumers can contact them if they believe they were victims of the alleged predatory practices related to opening loot boxes in Counter-Strike, Dota 2, or Team Fortress 2.

Much like the New York lawsuit, Berman is targeting the fact that children can sometimes be the ones opening loot boxes, therefore being exposed to gambling practices early on in life.

“What makes this case particularly egregious is that Valve knew children were on the other end of these transactions,” said Berman. “Rather than protect young players through age verification or a parental consent mechanism, we believe they rigged the game to extract more money from them.”

Learn more about the complaint at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to bring the latest information on this matter as more news develops.

How do you feel about these lawsuits against Valve? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Until Dawn Remake Developer Ballistic Moon Officially Dissolved
item.content:encoded:
The developer of Until Dawn Remake, Ballistic Moon, has officially been dissolved. Paperwork was filed with the UK government in February 2026.

Insider Gaming first exclusively reported in March 2025 that Until Dawn Remake developer Ballistic Moon had “effectively closed.” This followed the layoffs announced in September 2024.

Now, the company has officially dissolved, according to a Final Gazette filed in February 2026. The Until Dawn remake was released in October 2024, and by December of that year, the company had quietly laid off the remaining staff it had kept on board to support the game after launch.

The remake received mixed reviews. Currently, it has a Metacritic score of 70. Unfortunately, that’s the only project the team ever released. The studio was founded by former Supermassive Games staff members Duncan Kershaw, Neil McEwan, and Chris Lamb in 2019.

No major representatives from Sony or Ballistic Moon have commented on the studio’s dissolution. As of the aforementioned March 2025 report, Insider Gaming attempted multiple times to contact Ballistic Moon representatives and Sony. Should any statements be made by any representatives of either party, this story will be updated.

Studio Shakeups Carry on Through Early 2026

Earlier today, Electronic Arts announced several layoffs across its Battlefield Studios group. Studios of all sizes continue to make massive restructurings in these challenging times. Highguard from Wildlight Entertainment will be shutting down later this week after downsizing the studio to keep a core number of staff members on to support the game.

Ubisoft has also recently undergone massive shake-ups, resulting in the cancellation of numerous titles. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about the dissolution of Ballistic Moon? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
